# Copilot Instructions

- Monorepo with npm workspaces: backend (NestJS) and frontend (Next.js App Router) plus shared types.
- Root workflows: `npm run dev:all` starts both apps with prefixed logs; `dev:backend`/`dev:frontend` run individually; `build:all`, `lint`, and `test` are workspace-aware.
- Backend entry boots Nest with CORS, sessions, JSON body limits, compression, global `ValidationPipe`, and `HttpExceptionFilter`; API prefix is `api/v1`; Swagger/Scalar docs are exposed at `/docs` in non-prod ([apps/backend/src/main.ts](apps/backend/src/main.ts#L1-L77)).
- Config uses `ConfigModule.forRoot({ envFilePath: '.env' })` but `start:dev` passes `.env.development`; DB settings are pulled from `DB_*` env vars; TypeORM auto-loads entities and `synchronize: true` by default ([apps/backend/src/app.module.ts](apps/backend/src/app.module.ts#L1-L36)).
- Health and root endpoints use JSON:API-style success helpers; mirror that shape for new controllers ([apps/backend/src/app.controller.ts](apps/backend/src/app.controller.ts#L1-L18), [apps/backend/src/common/utils/http-response.ts](apps/backend/src/common/utils/http-response.ts#L1-L200)).
- Error handling: global filter wraps thrown errors into `{ status: { code, message }, errors: [...] }`, with special handling for class-validator arrays and preformatted error lists—prefer throwing `HttpException`/`BadRequestException` to benefit from it ([apps/backend/src/common/filters/http-exception/http-exception.filter.ts](apps/backend/src/common/filters/http-exception/http-exception.filter.ts#L1-L176)).
- JSON:API helpers include collection, paginated, created, validation-error, and attribute-extraction utilities; reuse instead of handcrafting responses ([apps/backend/src/common/utils/http-response.ts](apps/backend/src/common/utils/http-response.ts#L1-L200)).
- Utility transformer shows pattern for small data mappers (e.g., field remapping, numeric comma formatting) ([apps/backend/src/common/utils/tw-transformer.ts](apps/backend/src/common/utils/tw-transformer.ts#L1-L20)).
- Frontend follows 4-layer flow: Layout (SSR) → Screen (SSR data fetch) → Container (SSR/CSR business logic) → Component (client UI). Example home stack: layout, screen, container, and two client components ([apps/frontend/app/layout.tsx](apps/frontend/app/layout.tsx#L1-L40), [apps/frontend/app/(screens)/home/page.tsx](apps/frontend/app/(screens)/home/page.tsx#L1-L6), [apps/frontend/src/containers/home/home.tsx](apps/frontend/src/containers/home/home.tsx#L1-L25), [apps/frontend/src/components/card-welcome/card-welcome.tsx](apps/frontend/src/components/card-welcome/card-welcome.tsx#L1-L24), [apps/frontend/src/components/button-counter/button-counter.tsx](apps/frontend/src/components/button-counter/button-counter.tsx#L1-L35)).
- Client components use `'use client'`; containers default to server components unless hooks/state are needed—add `'use client'` sparingly per README guidance.
- Styling is Tailwind-first; layout already injects Geist fonts and dark-mode friendly classes.
- Shared domain types live in `packages/shared-types` and are published via workspace `shared-types`; import `User` etc. instead of redefining ([packages/shared-types/src/index.ts](packages/shared-types/src/index.ts#L1-L3), [packages/shared-types/src/model/user/index.ts](packages/shared-types/src/model/user/index.ts#L1-L6)).
- API surface currently minimal; keep new endpoints under `api/v1`, register modules in `AppModule`, and rely on TypeORM `entities` glob for models.
- Testing: backend uses Jest via `npm run test` in `apps/backend`; follow Nest testing conventions. Frontend currently has template defaults—add tests as needed.
- When adding configuration, remember sessions expect `SESSION_SECRET` and `APP_PORT`; update Swagger server URL if port changes.
- Prefer using provided response helpers and filters to keep consistent JSON:API envelopes and error shape across services.
